title: Fix `podman login` lying problem
body:
`podman login` lied when it doesn't login successfully

```
$ podman logout -a docker.io
Removed login credentials for all registries
$ podman login docker.io
Authenticating with existing credentials...
Existing credentials are valid. Already logged in to docker.io
$ podman logout docker.io
Error: error logging out of "docker.io": error updating "/run/user/1000/containers/auth.json": not logged in

```
`docker.CheckAuth` doesn't return error if `userFromAuthFile, passFromAuthFile` are `""`can lead to this always successful problem.

https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/ae8cc41295c4ff6f6f82372221c66c250691e4f6/cmd/podman/login.go#L115

Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Does this fix a logged issue?  I'm able to see this error against docker.io, but I'm not seeing the error when I login to a local registry:

```
# podman login --cert-dir /root/auth localhost:5000
Authenticating with existing credentials...
Existing credentials are invalid, please enter valid username and password
Username:
```

Still testing.
@TomSweeneyRedHat  there's no logged issue about this :eyes:

LGTM
FWIW, I figured out the discrepancy.   Apparently a value username/password combo for docker.io is ""/"".  That's not at all valid for a local registry unless you go through some hoops.  I'm not sure why they have theirs set up like that.  Anywho, the change cleans stuff up, nice catch/fix @QiWang19
